name: Summer Circus Spectacular
venue: Historic Asolo Theater at The Ringling
hours_label: Tue-Fri 11 AM & 2 PM, Sat 2 PM & 5 PM - $20 adult / $15 child
blurb: The Circus Arts Conservatory and The Ringling present an air-conditioned, hour-long showcase of world-class jugglers, aerialists, hand balancers and clowns in the intimate Historic Asolo Theater. A Sarasota summer tradition and a perfect family outing. Tickets are $20 for adults and $15 for kids 12 and under; add the Circus Museum for $5.

name: Sights + Sounds: Pops Orchestra Jazz Combo
blurb: A five-piece jazz combo from The Pops Orchestra brings soulful jazz and timeless American Songbook classics to the lakefront at Waterside Place. Free to attend and family-friendly, with restaurants and shops steps away. A relaxed way to spend a Friday evening on the water.

name: Sarasota Farmers Market
address: Lemon Ave & Main St, Sarasota, FL 34236
blurb: Downtown Sarasota's beloved open-air market fills Lemon Avenue and Main Street with local produce, seafood, baked goods, flowers, plants and artisan crafts every Saturday, rain or shine. Free to browse and a great way to sample the region's food scene. A weekly favorite for locals and visitors alike.

name: Siesta Key Drum Circle
blurb: Every Sunday, drummers, dancers and onlookers gather on Siesta Key's famous quartz-sand beach for a free, spontaneous drum circle that builds as the sun goes down. Bring a chair or just your feet and enjoy one of the Gulf Coast's most iconic sunsets. Family-friendly and completely free.
